title: Tempo Repeats - Uphill
descriptionMd:
## Tempo Repeats - Uphill

First hard session of the TempoRun phase. Attack these while you're fresh.

### Structure
- **Warmup:** 15 min easy + 4 strides
- **3 x Uphill Tempo** at RPE 8 (15/15/10 min) with 5 min easy jog recovery
- **Cooldown:** 10 min easy jog

### Notes
- Find a sustained climb of 6-8% grade
- Breathing should be deep and labored but controlled
- Pace will be slower than flat tempo; that's expected
- Focus on consistent effort, not pace

### Nutrition
- Hydrate well beforehand
- No fueling needed during this session (under 90 min)

title: Back-to-Back Tempo - Flat
descriptionMd:
## Back-to-Back Tempo - Flat

Second consecutive tempo day. Expect your legs to feel heavy. The point is to run on tired legs and accumulate training stress.

### Structure
- **Warmup:** 15 min easy + 4 strides
- **3 x Flat Tempo** (12/12/10 min) at RPE 8 with 3 min easy jog
- **Cooldown:** 10 min easy jog

### Notes
- Quality will be lower than yesterday; that is intentional
- Road or flat trail
- If RPE hits 9 early, shorten intervals rather than pushing through

title: Easy Recovery
descriptionMd:
## Easy Recovery

Loosen up the legs. Keep this genuinely easy.

- RPE 4-5, full conversation pace
- Flat to rolling terrain
- 40 minutes, no more
- Focus on relaxed form and breathing

title: Endurance Run - Trail
descriptionMd:
## Endurance Run - Trail

Steady aerobic effort on trails. This is your "forever pace" - sustainable from start to finish.

- RPE 5-6, rhythmic breathing
- Choose rolling trail with moderate elevation
- Build vertical fitness by staying on trails
- Conversation possible but not effortless

nutrition.notes: Bring water; no fueling needed under 90 min

title: Tempo - Progressive

intervals.description: Start at lower end of tempo effort
Build to full LT effort for the second half

descriptionMd:
## Tempo - Progressive

A single continuous tempo effort that builds in intensity.

### Structure
- **Warmup:** 15 min easy + 6 strides
- **Progressive Tempo:** 40 min total
  - First 20 min at RPE 7 (lower end of tempo range)
  - Second 20 min at RPE 8 (full LT effort)
- **Cooldown:** 10 min easy

### Notes
- Teaches pacing discipline
- Resist the urge to start too hard
- Road or smooth trail preferred for consistent effort

title: Long Run - Mountain Trail
descriptionMd:
## Long Run - Mountain Trail

The cornerstone of the week. Build time on feet and vertical on mountainous terrain.

- RPE 5-6 throughout
- Target 1000m+ of elevation gain
- Hike the steepest climbs; run everything else
- Practice race-day nutrition strategy

### Nutrition Plan
- Start fueling at 60 min mark
- Target 200-250 cal/hr from 60 min onward
- Carry 40-60 oz of water
- Test race-day foods (gels, bars, real food)

nutrition.notes: Practice race nutrition from 60 min onward

title: Back-to-Back Long - Easy Trail
descriptionMd:
## Back-to-Back Long - Easy Trail

The second leg of a back-to-back weekend. Your legs will feel heavy; that's the point.

- RPE 5, truly easy
- Run on trails to practice technical footing on tired legs
- Keep it conversational
- If legs are destroyed from Saturday, reduce to 60 min

### Notes
- This mimics the fatigue of miles 60-80 in a 100-miler
- Do not push the pace
- Focus on smooth, efficient movement

nutrition.notes: Practice nutrition; eat early and consistently
